socialTrends.name: Micron Technology
socialTrends.summary: Heavy focus on severe global HBM shortages and Micron's $100B long-term contract pipeline. Speculation on South Korean insider sells of DRAM is counteracted by intense bullish dip-buying expectations.

socialTrends.name: Meta Platforms
socialTrends.summary: Massive debates regarding Meta's $135B AI expenditure and potential new cloud computing arm. Speculation is high on whether options-led semiconductor volatility presents a buying opportunity.

socialTrends.name: SanDisk
socialTrends.summary: Retail traders are celebrating massive gains on SanDisk calls, heavily driven by DRAM memory pricing increases, though some caution against a near-term semiconductor top.

socialTrends.name: SpaceX
socialTrends.summary: Extremely high hype as retail investors track SpaceX's spectacular post-IPO performance and impending inclusion into the Nasdaq 100 on July 7, 2026, though some debate its massive $2.16T valuation.

socialTrends.name: Tesla
socialTrends.summary: Intense retail frustration as the stock suffered a sharp correction despite beating delivery numbers. Debates center on near-term margin pressure, EV delivery fatigue, and Optimus Gen 3 timelines.

socialTrends.name: NVIDIA
socialTrends.summary: Active discussion on whether NVDA's current level represents a key support floor or if options-led volatility will drag the stock lower. Long-term bulls continue to highlight its AI and physical robotics dominance.
